# terrible_community_aws_autoscaling_lifecycle_hook (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `autoscaling_group_name` (String) The name of the Auto Scaling group to which you want to assign the lifecycle hook.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `lifecycle_hook_name` (String) The name of the lifecycle hook.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `default_result` (String) Defines the action the Auto Scaling group should take when the lifecycle hook timeout elapses or if an unexpected failure occurs.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `heartbeat_timeout` (Number) The amount of time, in seconds, that can elapse before the lifecycle hook times out. When the lifecycle hook times out, Auto Scaling performs the default action. You can prevent the lifecycle hook from timing out by calling RecordLifecycleActionHeartbeat. By default Amazon AWS will use `3600` (1 hour).
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `notification_meta_data` (String) Contains additional information that you want to include any time Auto Scaling sends a message to the notification target.
- `notification_target_arn` (String) The ARN of the notification target that Auto Scaling will use to notify you when an instance is in the transition state for the lifecycle hook. This target can be either an SQS queue or an SNS topic. If you specify an empty string, this overrides the current ARN.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `role_arn` (String) The ARN of the IAM role that allows the Auto Scaling group to publish to the specified notification target.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Create or delete Lifecycle Hook. When *state=present* updates existing hook or creates a new hook if not found.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `transition` (String) The instance state to which you want to attach the lifecycle hook. Required when *state=present*.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `auto_scaling_group_name` (String) The unique name of the auto scaling group.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `global_timeout` (Number) The maximum time, in seconds, that an instance can remain in a `Pending:Wait` or `Terminating:Wait` state.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `lifecycle_transition` (String) The instance state to which lifecycle hook should be attached.
